task_completion.description: Open the correct file in WPS Spreadsheet
task_completion.visual_evidence: The WPS Spreadsheet application is open with 'residential_takeoff.xlsx' visible in the title bar or tab.
On the 'Project_Takeoff' sheet, Column D ('Description') and Column E ('CSI_Division') are filled with text data retrieved from the price book.

task_completion.description: Calculate adjusted quantities and line item costs
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the 'Project_Takeoff' sheet, Columns F ('Adj_Material_Qty'), G ('Mat_Cost_Total'), H ('Lab_Cost_Total'), and I ('Line_Total') are populated with numerical values.

task_completion.description: Create a Bid_Summary sheet summarizing costs by CSI_Division
task_completion.visual_evidence: A sheet tab named 'Bid_Summary' is visible at the bottom. The sheet contains a table listing unique CSI Divisions and their aggregated total costs.

task_completion.description: Calculate Subtotal, 15% Markup, and Final Total Bid Price
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Bid_Summary' sheet displays a Subtotal (approx $17,854.50), a 15% 'Overhead & Profit' markup, and a Final Total Bid Price of exactly $20,532.68.

task_completion.description: Format all cost columns as Currency
task_completion.visual_evidence: Cost values in Columns G, H, and I on the 'Project_Takeoff' sheet, as well as the aggregated costs and totals on the 'Bid_Summary' sheet, display the Currency symbol ($).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the WPS Spreadsheet GUI rather than using a terminal script to modify the file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the WPS Spreadsheet application interface being actively used, with menus, ribbons, or cell selections visible.

integrity.description: The agent used spreadsheet formulas rather than hardcoding the final answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: When a calculated cell is selected, the formula bar (fx) shows a formula (e.g., VLOOKUP, SUMIF, or arithmetic operations) rather than a raw typed number.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely created the summary table rather than just typing the final expected total.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Bid_Summary' sheet contains a structured table with multiple rows for different CSI Divisions, leading up to the subtotal and final total, rather than just a single cell with the final answer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct final construction bid estimate total is exactly $20,532.68. This is derived from a subtotal of $17,854.50 and a 15% Overhead & Profit markup. The final spreadsheet must have exactly three tabs: 'Master_Price_Book', 'Project_Takeoff', and 'Bid_Summary'. The agent is expected to use formulas such as VLOOKUP (or INDEX/XLOOKUP) and SUMIF to complete the task.
